naturalLanguage: Igloo construction is a kind of construction process — a structured process of building a shelter from snow blocks according to established engineering principles.
An igloo is a kind of shelter — a structure providing protection from the environment.
The caribou clothing cycle is a kind of manufacturing process — converting raw animal hide into functional garments through a structured sequence of preparation, cutting, and sewing stages.

The caribou clothing cycle is a kind of heritage skill — traditional knowledge transmitted across generations that embodies Pilimmaksarniq (learning through practice) and Pijitsirniq (serving the community).
A qulliq is an artifact — a human-made object (carved from soapstone) that serves specific functional purposes: providing light, heat, and cooking capability.
Qulliq operation involves a controlled combustion process — the oxidation of seal oil producing light and heat through a shaped wick.
Ilgaak (snow goggles) are an artifact — carved from bone, ivory, or antler with narrow horizontal slits to reduce UV light exposure and prevent snow blindness.
Ilgaak (snow goggles) are a kind of wearable item — protective eyewear designed to be worn over the eyes to prevent snow blindness.
A qamutik (dog sled) is a kind of vehicle — a human-constructed transport device used for travel across snow and ice.
A qamutik is a kind of transportation device — specifically designed for hauling loads across arctic snow and sea ice terrain.
Inuit waterproof sewing is a kind of manufacturing process — applying a specialized crimped stitch technique to compress hide fibers and create a water-resistant seal at garment seams.
Inuit waterproof sewing is a kind of heritage skill — traditional knowledge with critical survival implications, transmitted through demonstration and practice across generations.
Dene snowshoes are an artifact — wooden-framed footwear strapped over boots to distribute weight on soft snow, enabling travel through boreal forest terrain. (Dene cross-reference)

Dene snowshoes are a kind of wearable item — attached to the feet to enable travel on snow that would otherwise be impassable. Multiple regional designs exist for different snow conditions. (Dene cross-reference)
